2026-04-20T20:45:13.8614521Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8615080Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8615492Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8615861Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8616166Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8616402Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8616556Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8616728Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8616892Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8617111Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T20:45:13.8617578Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T20:45:13.8617826Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. 2026-04-20T20:48:44.4917509Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T20:46:19.355603485Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58404,\"title\":\"[ResourceManager] Docs: Document AddArmClient IHostApplicationBuilder extensions\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58123) by `@m-nash`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58123 added a new `ArmClientHostExtensions` class with four public extension methods that register `ArmClient` as a singleton in an `IHostApplicationBuilder`\\u0026#39;s service collection using the Azure client factory pattern:\\n\\n- `AddArmClient(host, sectionName)` — registers a singleton `ArmClient` from a configuration section\\n- `AddArmClient(host, sectionName, configureSettings)` — same, with a settings post-processing callback\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(host, key, sectionName)` — registers a keyed singleton `ArmClient`\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(host, key, sectionName, configureSettings)` — same, with a settings post-processing callback\\n\\nThese are marked `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]` and live in `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Extensions/ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README.md has no dependency-injection section.** The README covers manual `new ArmClient(...)` construction and manual authentication but has no guidance for `IHostApplicationBuilder` / .NET Generic Host DI registration. The new extension methods are not referenced anywhere in the README.\\n- **CHANGELOG.md `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` Features Added section is empty.** The `AddArmClient` / `AddKeyedArmClient` additions were not logged in the upcoming release notes.\\n- **No sample code or test snippet.** There is no `tests/Samples/` directory under `Azure.ResourceManager` and no snippet showing how to call `AddArmClient` in a `Program.cs` / host startup context.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** (edit)\\n\\nUnder the `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` heading, replace the empty `### Features Added` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder`\\n  to register `ArmClient` as a singleton in the .NET Generic Host service collection.\\n  Configuration is read from the specified `IConfiguration` section name using `ArmClientSettings`.\\n  These methods are experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`).\\n```\\n\\n**File 2 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new **\\u0026#34;Use with dependency injection\\u0026#34;** subsection inside the existing `## Getting started` section, after the `### Authenticate the Client` subsection. Content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Use with dependency injection\\n\\nIf your application uses the .NET Generic Host (ASP.NET Core, Worker Service, etc.), you can\\nregister `ArmClient` with the DI container using the `AddArmClient` extension on\\n`IHostApplicationBuilder`. This API is currently experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`).\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite the sample code in a **new** test file:\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_HostedService.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_HostedService\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AddArmClientToHost()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ResourceManager_AddArmClient\\n            // Build an IHostApplicationBuilder (e.g. WebApplication.CreateBuilder in ASP.NET Core)\\n            IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\\n\\n            // Register ArmClient using the \\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34; section of appsettings.json.\\n            // The section should contain at minimum a \\u0026#34;TenantId\\u0026#34; and a \\u0026#34;SubscriptionId\\u0026#34;.\\n            builder.AddArmClient(\\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Services that depend on ArmClient can now receive it via constructor injection.\\n            IHost host = builder.Build();\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            Assert.IsNotNull(host);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n- **Snippet name:** `ResourceManager_AddArmClient`\\n- **README location:** the new `### Use with dependency injection` subsection added in Step 1\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the `Getting started` section needs the new DI subsection.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcemanager` — injects the `ResourceManager_AddArmClient` snippet from the test file into the README placeholder\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcemanager` — not strictly needed here (no public API surface changed by docs), but run to confirm API listings are clean\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24689149831/agentic_workflow) · ● 397.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58404\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T20:43:26Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T20:43:26Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The new extension methods are not referenced anywhere in the README.\\n- **CHANGELOG.md `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` Features Added section is empty.** The `AddArmClient` / `AddKeyedArmClient` additions were not logged in the upcoming release notes.\\n- **No sample code or test snippet.** There is no `tests/Samples/` directory under `Azure.ResourceManager` and no snippet showing how to call `AddArmClient` in a `Program.cs` / host startup context.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** (edit)\\n\\nUnder the `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` heading, replace the empty `### Features Added` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder`\\n  to register `ArmClient` as a singleton in the .NET Generic Host service collection.\\n  Configuration is read from the specified `IConfiguration` section name using `ArmClientSettings`.\\n  These methods are experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`).\\n```\\n\\n**File 2 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new **\\u0026#34;Use with dependency injection\\u0026#34;** subsection inside the existing `## Getting started` section, after the `### Authenticate the Client` subsection. This API is currently experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`).\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite the sample code in a **new** test file:\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_HostedService.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. 2026-04-20T20:48:44.5074833Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T20:46:29.95377763Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":40,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":3402551858,\"number\":52558,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] No registered resource provider found for location 'westeurope' and API version '2025-03-01' for type 'publicIPAddresses'.\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager 1.13.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWe are using the SDK to delete different kinds of resources (VMs, KVs, disks, etc.):\\n```\\nawait resource.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Started);\\n```\\nToday this call started to fail consistently with 400 Bad Request when deleting public IPs, network security groups and network interface cards. The error mentions a missing resource provider for a recent API version, but the resources were created via an ARM template using `2020-07-01`, while the SDK doesn't expose which version it uses.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe method call to succeed.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nWhen deleting PIPs (same for NSGs and NICs) we get the following error message:\\n```\\nNo registered resource provider found for location 'westeurope' and API version '2025-03-01' for type 'publicIPAddresses'. The supported api-versions are '2014-12-01-preview, 2015-05-01-preview, 2015-06-15, 2016-03-30, 2016-06-01, 2016-07-01, 2016-08-01, 2016-09-01, 2016-10-01, 2016-11-01, 2016-12-01, 2017-03-01, 2017-04-01, 2017-06-01, 2017-08-01, 2017-09-01, 2017-10-01, 2017-11-01, 2018-01-01, 2018-02-01, 2018-03-01, 2018-04-01, 2018-05-01, 2018-06-01, 2018-07-01, 2018-08-01, 2018-10-01, 2018-11-01, 2018-12-01, 2019-02-01, 2019-04-01, 2019-06-01, 2019-07-01, 2019-08-01, 2019-09-01, 2019-11-01, 2019-12-01, 2020-01-01, 2020-03-01, 2020-04-01, 2020-05-01, 2020-06-01, 2020-07-01, 2020-08-01, 2020-11-01, 2021-01-01, 2021-02-01, 2021-03-01, 2021-04-01, 2021-05-01, 2021-06-01, 2021-08-01, 2021-12-01, 2022-01-01, 2022-05-01, 2022-07-01, 2022-09-01, 2022-11-01, 2023-02-01, 2023-04-01, 2023-05-01, 2023-06-01, 2023-09-01, 2023-11-01, 2024-01-01, 2024-03-01, 2024-05-01, 2024-07-01, 2024-10-01, 2025-01-01'. The supported locations are 'westus, eastus, northeurope, westeurope, eastasia, southeastasia, northcentralus, southcentralus, centralus, eastus2, japaneast, japanwest, brazilsouth, australiaeast, australiasoutheast, centralindia, southindia, westindia, canadacentral, canadaeast, westcentralus, westus2, ukwest, uksouth, koreacentral, koreasouth, francecentral, australiacentral, southafricanorth, uaenorth, switzerlandnorth, germanywestcentral, norwayeast, westus3, jioindiawest, swedencentral, qatarcentral, polandcentral, italynorth, israelcentral, mexicocentral, spaincentral, newzealandnorth, indonesiacentral, austriaeast, chilecentral, malaysiawest'.\\n```\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```\\nvar resourceGroupName = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar resourcesPrefix = \\\"abc\\\";\\n\\nvar subscriptionId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar tenantId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar clientId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar clientSecret = \\\"abc\\\";\\n\\nvar credentials = new ClientSecretCredential(\\n    tenantId,\\n    clientId,\\n    clientSecret);\\nvar armClient = new ArmClient(credentials);\\n\\nstring resourceGroupId = $\\\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}\\\";\\nvar rg = armClient.GetResourceGroupResource(ResourceIdentifier.Parse(resourceGroupId))?.Get()?.Value;\\nvar resources = rg?.GetGenericResources($\\\"substringof(\\\\'{resourcesPrefix}\\\\', name)\\\", null, 20).ToList();\\n\\nforeach (var resource in resources)\\n{\\n    try\\n    {\\n        await resource.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Started);\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\\"Deleting resource: {resource.Id}\\\");\\n    }\\n    catch (Exception ex)\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\\"Error processing resource {resource.Id}: {ex.Message}\\\");\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No RequestFailedException ErrorCode null with net6.0 and System.Text.Json 9+\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager 1.13.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWe have a helper function that is used all over our code that looks similar to below. It recently broke when updating `System.Text.Json` to version 9+. We are stuck on .NET 6 for the time so are combining this with up to date `System.Text.Json` versions. Some combination of these two changes the behavior of `RequestFailedException` parsing.\\n\\n- **.NET6, System.Text.Json 9+**: Does not work\\n- **.NET8, System.Text.Json 9+**: Works!!\\n- **.NET6, System.Text.Json 8**: Works!!\\n\\nThe `ErrorCode` component of `RequestFailedException` is always null - debugging into the SDK shows that it's using `System.Text.Json` to construct the exception from the response.\\n\\nAre there any known compatibility issues between these versions of framework/packages? \\n\\n```csharp\\nbool IsError(RequestFailedException exception, int statusCode, string errorCode, ILogger logger)\\n{\\n    if (exception != null)\\n    {\\n        bool statusMatch = exception.Status == statusCode;\\n        // Use case-insensitive comparison for ErrorCode matching as in original code\\n        bool errorCodeMatch = string.Equals(exception.ErrorCode, errorCode, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\\n\\n        // Use the passed-in logger\\n        logger.LogDebug($\\\"IsError Check: Target(Status={statusCode}, Code='{errorCode}') | Actual(Status={exception.Status}, Code='{exception.ErrorCode ?? \\\"\\u003cNULL\\u003e\\\"}') | StatusMatch={statusMatch}, CodeMatch={errorCodeMatch}\\\");\\n\\n        return statusMatch \\u0026\\u0026 errorCodeMatch;\\n    }\\n    else\\n    {\\n        logger.LogDebug(\\\"IsError Check: Exception was null.\\\");\\n        return false;\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThank you so much!\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`ErrorCode` should have a text description of the API call, like `SubscriptionNotFound`, `ResourceNotFound`, `ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked`, etc for .NET6 and `System.Text.Json` 9+.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n`ErrorCode` is always null\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n**Program.cs**\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\\n\\n// --- Configuration ---\\n// IMPORTANT: Set your Azure Subscription ID here or via Environment Variable\\nconst string AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID_ENV_VAR = \\\"AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\\";\\nstring subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID_ENV_VAR) ?? \\\"\\u003cYOUR SUB\\u003e\\\"; // \\u003c--- CHANGE THIS if not using Env Var\\nstring resourceGroupName = $\\\"nonexistent-rg-{Guid.NewGuid()}\\\"; // Guarantees it doesn't exist\\n// ---------------------\\n\\n// --- Set up Logging ---\\nusing var loggerFactory = LoggerFactory.Create(builder =\\u003e\\n{\\n    builder.AddConsole();\\n    // Optional: Set minimum log level if needed\\n    // builder.SetMinimumLevel(LogLevel.Debug);\\n});\\n// Create the logger instance that will be passed
